title: Pleurothyrium glandulosum
intro: Pleurothyrium glandulosum is a plant species in the family Annonaceae, native to the Amazon rainforest. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including antimicrobial, antiparasitic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which may be useful in treating a range of health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pleurothyrium glandulosum include annonaceous acetogenins, which are a group of compounds with potential therapeutic applications. In traditional medicine, Pleurothyrium glandulosum is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Pleurothyrium glandulosum is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. As a member of the Annonaceae family, it is closely related to other plants such as custard apples and soursop. The classification of Pleurothyrium glandulosum places it within the order Magnoliales, a group of flowering plants characterized by their distinctive flowers and seeds. Further classification of the plant also includes its placement within the genus Pleurothyrium, which contains several other species of plants.

Additionally, Pleurothyrium glandulosum has been used in folk medicine to treat gastrointestinal issues and as a natural remedy for wound healing.
